perturbation.original_text: 1.99 "Licensed Compound" means (a) the compound known as HPN217 (as described on Schedule 1.99), [***].
perturbation.changed_text: 1.99 "Licensed Compound" means (a) the compound known as HPN217 (as described on Schedule 1.99), [***]. Notwithstanding the foregoing, for the purposes of royalty payments outlined in Article 6, a Licensed Compound shall only be defined as such if it receives Regulatory Approval from the FDA.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of "Licensed Compound" is broad, encompassing HPN217. The added text restricts this definition for royalty payment purposes, requiring FDA approval. This introduces an in-text contradiction because the term "Licensed Compound" is used throughout the agreement with the initial, broader definition. Now, any clause discussing royalties is subject to interpretation as to whether it refers to the broader or narrower definition of “Licensed Compound”, creating enforcement uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text:
6.5 Royalties.
6.5.1 Royalty Rates. As further consideration for the rights granted to AbbVie hereunder, subject to Section 6.5.3, commencing upon the First Commercial Sale of a Licensed Product in the Territory, on a Licensed Product- by-Licensed Product basis, AbbVie shall pay to Harpoon a royalty on Net Sales of each Licensed Product in the Territory (excluding Net Sales of each Licensed Product in any country or other jurisdiction in the Territory for which the Royalty Term for such Licensed Product in such country or other jurisdiction has expired) during [***] at the following rates:
Net Sales in the Territory of each Licensed Product in a [***]
Royalty Rate
For that portion of aggregate Net Sales of each Licensed Product[***] [***]
For that portion of aggregate Net Sales of each Licensed Product[***] [***]
For that portion of aggregate Net Sales of each Licensed Product[***] [***]
With respect to each Licensed Product in each country or other jurisdiction in the Territory, [***].
perturbation.justification: The original definition of "Licensed Compound" is broadened to include HPN217 regardless of FDA approval. However, the changed text adds a caveat: for royalty payments in Article 6, a Licensed Compound requires FDA approval. This directly contradicts the broad definition, making royalty calculations based on 'Licensed Product' (derived from 'Licensed Compound') ambiguous. Section 6.5 (Royalties) relies on the original, broader definition, but the modified text introduces a narrower definition specifically for royalty purposes, which directly conflicts with Section 6.5.1, where royalties are calculated on Net Sales of each 'Licensed Product'. This creates a clear in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 12.2.1 Material Breach. If either Party (the "Non-Breaching Party") believes that the other Party (the "Breaching Party") has materially breached one (1) or more of its material obligations under this Agreement, then the Non-Breaching Party may deliver notice of such material breach to the Breaching Party (a "Default Notice"). If the Breaching Party does not dispute that it has committed a material breach of one (1) or more of its material obligations under this Agreement, then if the Breaching Party fails to cure such breach within ninety (90) days after receipt of the Default Notice, or if such compliance cannot be fully achieved within such ninety- (90-) day period and the Breaching Party has failed to commence compliance or has failed to use diligent efforts to achieve full compliance as soon thereafter as is reasonably possible, the Non-Breaching Party may terminate this Agreement upon written notice to the Breaching Party. If the Breaching Party disputes that it has materially breached one (1) or more of its material obligations under this Agreement, the dispute shall be resolved pursuant to Section 13.7. If, as a result of the application of such dispute resolution procedures, the Breaching Party is determined to be in material breach of one (1) or more of its material obligations under this Agreement (an "Adverse Ruling"), then if the Breaching Party fails to complete the actions specified by the Adverse Ruling to cure such material breach within [***] after such ruling, or if such compliance cannot be fully achieved within such [***] period and the Breaching Party has failed to commence diligent efforts to achieve full compliance as soon thereafter as is reasonably possible or as prescribed by the Arbitrator, then the Non-Breaching Party may terminate this Agreement upon written notice to the Breaching Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 12.2.1 Material Breach. If either Party (the "Non-Breaching Party") believes that the other Party (the "Breaching Party") has materially breached one (1) or more of its material obligations under this Agreement, then the Non-Breaching Party may deliver notice of such material breach to the Breaching Party (a "Default Notice"). If the Breaching Party fails to cure such breach within ninety (90) days after receipt of the Default Notice, the Non-Breaching Party may terminate this Agreement upon written notice to the Breaching Party. If the Breaching Party disputes that it has materially breached one (1) or more of its material obligations under this Agreement, the dispute shall be resolved pursuant to Section 13.7. If, as a result of the application of such dispute resolution procedures, the Breaching Party is determined to be in material breach of one (1) or more of its material obligations under this Agreement (an "Adverse Ruling"), then if the Breaching Party fails to complete the actions specified by the Adverse Ruling to cure such material breach within [***] after such ruling, then the Non-Breaching Party may terminate this Agreement upon written notice to the Breaching Party.
perturbation.explanation: In the original text, the Breaching Party had the opportunity to commence compliance or use diligent efforts in order to potentially extend the timeline to cure the breach. The modified version removes the following: "or if such compliance cannot be fully achieved within such ninety- (90-) day period and the Breaching Party has failed to commence compliance or has failed to use diligent efforts to achieve full compliance as soon thereafter as is reasonably possible." as well as "or if such compliance cannot be fully achieved within such [***] period and the Breaching Party has failed to commence diligent efforts to achieve full compliance as soon thereafter as is reasonably possible or as prescribed by the Arbitrator,". This introduces an in-text contradiction and enforcement uncertainty because if compliance can't be fully achieved in the allocated time but the Breaching Party is actively doing all possible to achieve compliance, in the original text there's language that allows to avoid termination due to material breach, which now doesn't exist in the changed version, contradicting the opportunity and protection granted by the original language.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12.2.1 Material Breach. If either Party (the "Non-Breaching Party") believes that the other Party (the "Breaching Party") has materially breached one (1) or more of its material obligations under this Agreement, then the Non-Breaching Party may deliver notice of such material breach to the Breaching Party (a "Default Notice"). If the Breaching Party does not dispute that it has committed a material breach of one (1) or more of its material obligations under this Agreement, then if the Breaching Party fails to cure such breach within ninety (90) days after receipt of the Default Notice, or if such compliance cannot be fully achieved within such ninety- (90-) day period and the Breaching Party has failed to commence compliance or has failed to use diligent efforts to achieve full compliance as soon thereafter as is reasonably possible, the Non-Breaching Party may terminate this Agreement upon written notice to the Breaching Party. If the Breaching Party disputes that it has materially breached one (1) or more of its material obligations under this Agreement, the dispute shall be resolved pursuant to Section 13.7. If, as a result of the application of such dispute resolution procedures, the Breaching Party is determined to be in material breach of one (1) or more of its material obligations under this Agreement (an "Adverse Ruling"), then if the Breaching Party fails to complete the actions specified by the Adverse Ruling to cure such material breach within [***] after such ruling, or if such compliance cannot be fully achieved within such [***] period and the Breaching Party has failed to commence diligent efforts to achieve full compliance as soon thereafter as is reasonably possible or as prescribed by the Arbitrator, then the Non-Breaching Party may terminate this Agreement upon written notice to the Breaching Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the allowance for the Breaching Party to avoid termination if they are diligently working to achieve full compliance, even if full compliance isn't possible within the initial timeframe. This directly contradicts the original text and the contradicted text which both include the provision for diligent efforts, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction as now the breaching party has no protections if they are diligently trying to resolve an issue.

perturbation.original_text: 3.4 Post-Exercise Development Activities. Following the License Option Exercise Closing Date, except for Harpoon's responsibilities in completing the Initial Development Activities and delivering the Final Development Report, AbbVie shall have the sole right to Develop and Manufacture (and shall control all aspects of Development and Manufacturing), including seeking Regulatory Approvals for, Licensed Compounds and Licensed Products in the Field and in the Territory and, for clarity, Harpoon and its Affiliates shall have no right to do so. Following the License Option Exercise Closing Date, AbbVie shall use Commercially Reasonable Efforts to Develop and obtain Regulatory Approval for [***] Licensed Product for [***] for use in [***] Major Market. AbbVie shall have the right to satisfy its diligence obligations under this Section 3.4 through its Affiliates or Sublicensees. Except as set forth in this Section 3.4, AbbVie shall have no other diligence obligations, express or implied, with respect to the Development of the Licensed Compounds or Licensed Products in the Territory. Following the License Option Exercise Closing Date and until the First Commercial Sale of a Licensed Product in a Major Market, AbbVie will provide to Harpoon following disbandment of the JGC, [***] reports within [***] after the end of each [***], in each case summarizing the key Development activities undertaken and summarizing the results achieved with respect to the applicable Licensed Compounds and Licensed Products in all Major Markets during such [***]. Prior to the disbandment of the JGC, AbbVie shall provide the JGC
perturbation.changed_text: 3.4 Post-Exercise Development Activities. Following the License Option Exercise Closing Date, except for Harpoon's responsibilities in completing the Initial Development Activities and delivering the Final Development Report, AbbVie shall have the sole right to Develop and Manufacture (and shall control all aspects of Development and Manufacturing), including seeking Regulatory Approvals for, Licensed Compounds and Licensed Products in the Field and in the Territory and, for clarity, Harpoon and its Affiliates shall have no right to do so. AbbVie shall use Commercially Reasonable Efforts to Develop and obtain Regulatory Approval for [***] Licensed Product for [***] for use in [***] Major Market. AbbVie shall have the right to satisfy its diligence obligations under this Section 3.4 through its Affiliates or Sublicensees. Except as set forth in this Section 3.4, AbbVie shall have no other diligence obligations, express or implied, with respect to the Development of the Licensed Compounds or Licensed Products in the Territory.  Notwithstanding the foregoing, AbbVie is under no circumstances required to expend more than $10,000 in Development costs per year on the Licensed Compounds or Licensed Products. Following the License Option Exercise Closing Date and until the First Commercial Sale of a Licensed Product in a Major Market, AbbVie will provide to Harpoon following disbandment of the JGC, [***] reports within [***] after the end of each [***], in each case summarizing the key Development activities undertaken and summarizing the results achieved with respect to the applicable Licensed Compounds and Licensed Products in all Major Markets during such [***]. Prior to the disbandment of the JGC, AbbVie shall provide the JGC
perturbation.explanation: By introducing a limitation that "AbbVie is under no circumstances required to expend more than $10,000 in Development costs per year on the Licensed Compounds or Licensed Products", a contradiction arises. The initial part of Section 3.4 asserts that AbbVie shall use Commercially Reasonable Efforts to Develop. However, this additional expenditure limit severely restricts AbbVie's Commercially Reasonable Efforts to a maximum of $10,000. There is an in-text contradiction with how AbbVie shall act for this Development activities, creating enforcement uncertainty on whether the Commercially Reasonable Efforts where being done correctly or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.4 Post-Exercise Development Activities. Following the License Option Exercise Closing Date, except for Harpoon's responsibilities in completing the Initial Development Activities and delivering the Final Development Report, AbbVie shall have the sole right to Develop and Manufacture (and shall control all aspects of Development and Manufacturing), including seeking Regulatory Approvals for, Licensed Compounds and Licensed Products in the Field and in the Territory and, for clarity, Harpoon and its Affiliates shall have no right to do so. AbbVie shall have the right to satisfy its diligence obligations under this Section 3.4 through its Affiliates or Sublicensees. Except as set forth in this Section 3.4, AbbVie shall have no other diligence obligations, express or implied, with respect to the Development of the Licensed Compounds or Licensed Products in the Territory. Following the License Option Exercise Closing Date and until the First Commercial Sale of a Licensed Product in a Major Market, AbbVie will provide to Harpoon following disbandment of the JGC, [***] reports within [***] after the end of each [***], in each case summarizing the key Development activities undertaken and summarizing the results achieved with respect to the applicable Licensed Compounds and Licensed Products in all Major Markets during such [***]. Prior to the disbandment of the JGC, AbbVie shall provide the JGC
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a new limitation on AbbVie's development costs, capping them at $10,000 per year. This directly contradicts the original text's requirement for AbbVie to use 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts to Develop'. A fixed, low-cost limit can prevent AbbVie from fulfilling what would normally be considered 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts,' creating a direct conflict between the obligation and the financial constraint as stated in Section 3.4.
